meaning: n.<美>(对囚犯的)一级防范禁闭 一级防范禁闭期
examples: Prisoners have been placed on lockdown to prevent further violence at the jail.
The lockdown affected 16 million people across the country.
Before the lockdown, my family went to my parents' hometown in Caidian, a suburb of Wuhan.
The eradication team remained in camp under a tight security lockdown for ten days.
Officials lifted the city-wide lockdown, telling citizens they were free to leave their homes again.
